test_cases.description: Server loads config.toml from the current working directory by default and applies all documented field defaults when no config file is present.
preconditions: No config.toml exists in the current working directory
No --config flag is provided
Server binary is available at bin/af-hub
test_cases.input: Start server without any --config flag and without a config.toml present
test_cases.expected: Server starts normally; configuration reflects defaults: port=8080, bind=0.0.0.0, db_path=./data/af-hub.db, log_level=info; no fatal error is logged; HTTP listener opens on 0.0.0.0:8080
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = loadConfig(configPath="./config.toml", flagProvided=false)
assert cfg.Server.Port == 8080
assert cfg.Server.Bind == "0.0.0.0"
assert cfg.Server.ExternalURL == ""
assert cfg.Database.Path == "./data/af-hub.db"
assert cfg.Log.Level == "info"
assert serverStartsWithoutError == true

test_cases.description: Server exits with code 1 and logs a fatal error when config.toml exists but contains invalid TOML syntax.
preconditions: config.toml exists at the resolved path containing malformed TOML (e.g., unclosed bracket `[server`)
test_cases.input: config.toml with content: `[server\nport = 8080` (malformed, unclosed bracket)
test_cases.expected: Process exits with code 1; a fatal-level structured JSON log entry is emitted to stdout containing the parse failure reason; no HTTP port is opened
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
write_file("./config.toml", "[server\nport = 8080")
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code == 1
assert parse_json_log_contains(result.stdout, {"level": "fatal"})
assert http_port_not_open(8080)

test_cases.description: Server logs a warn-level entry per unrecognized field and continues startup when config.toml contains unknown fields or section headers.
preconditions: config.toml exists with unrecognized fields such as `[servr]` (typo) and an unknown key `foo = bar`
test_cases.input: config.toml containing: `[servr]\nport = 9090\nfoo = "bar"`
test_cases.expected: Server starts normally; one warn-level structured log entry is emitted to stdout for each unrecognized field including its name/path; defaults are applied for unrecognized keys
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
write_file("./config.toml", "[servr]\nport = 9090\nfoo = \"bar\"")
result = start_server_successfully()
warn_logs = filter_logs(result.stdout, level="warn")
assert len(warn_logs) >= 1
for each unrecognized_field in ["servr", "foo"]:
  assert any(log contains field_name_or_path=unrecognized_field for log in warn_logs)
assert server_is_listening() == true

test_cases.description: Server logs a warn-level entry with the invalid_value field and defaults log level to info when config.toml specifies an unrecognized log.level.
preconditions: config.toml exists with `[log]\nlevel = "verbose"` (not a recognized logrus level)
test_cases.input: config.toml containing: `[log]\nlevel = "verbose"`
test_cases.expected: Server starts normally using info log level; one warn-level JSON log entry is emitted with field `invalid_value` set to `verbose`; no fatal error occurs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
write_file("./config.toml", "[log]\nlevel = \"verbose\"")
result = start_server_successfully()
warn_log = find_log_entry(result.stdout, level="warn", msg_contains="unrecognized log level")
assert warn_log != nil
assert warn_log["invalid_value"] == "verbose"
assert effective_log_level() == "info"

test_cases.description: Server correctly parses all documented config fields from a fully populated config.toml and applies them.
preconditions: A fully populated config.toml is present with all recognized fields set to non-default values
test_cases.input: config.toml with port=9090, bind=127.0.0.1, external_url=https://example.com, database.path=./custom/db.db, log.level=debug, and one [[oauth.providers]] block with name=github, client_id=abc, client_secret=xyz
test_cases.expected: Parsed config struct has Server.Port=9090, Server.Bind=127.0.0.1, Server.ExternalURL=https://example.com, Database.Path=./custom/db.db, Log.Level=debug, OAuth.Providers[0].Name=github, ClientID=abc, ClientSecret=xyz; server starts on port 9090
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
write_file("./config.toml", full_config_content)
cfg = loadConfig("./config.toml")
assert cfg.Server.Port == 9090
assert cfg.Server.Bind == "127.0.0.1"
assert cfg.Server.ExternalURL == "https://example.com"
assert cfg.Database.Path == "./custom/db.db"
assert cfg.Log.Level == "debug"
assert len(cfg.OAuth.Providers) == 1
assert cfg.OAuth.Providers[0].Name == "github"
assert cfg.OAuth.Providers[0].ClientID == "abc"
assert cfg.OAuth.Providers[0].ClientSecret == "xyz"

test_cases.description: Server initializes all subsystems in strict sequential order during startup.
preconditions: Clean environment: no existing database, no config.toml, no AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN env var
Server binary available at bin/af-hub
test_cases.input: Start server without any flags in a clean directory
test_cases.expected: Log entries appear in stdout in strict order: logging setup before DB messages, DB init before admin bootstrap messages, admin bootstrap before route registration, startup info log entry appears immediately before server begins accepting connections; no step is skipped or reordered
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = capture_startup_logs(run_server())
log_timestamps = extract_log_sequence(result.stdout)
assert index_of(log_timestamps, "db init") > index_of(log_timestamps, "logging initialized")
assert index_of(log_timestamps, "admin bootstrap") > index_of(log_timestamps, "db init")
assert index_of(log_timestamps, "server starting") > index_of(log_timestamps, "admin bootstrap")
assert server_accepts_connections_only_after_log("server starting")

test_cases.description: Server emits a single info-level structured log entry with bind, port, db_path, log_level, and msg=server starting immediately before the HTTP listener starts.
preconditions: Clean environment with no config.toml; server is starting for the first time
test_cases.input: Start server with default configuration
test_cases.expected: Exactly one JSON log entry at info level with msg=server starting, bind=0.0.0.0, port=8080 (integer), db_path=./data/af-hub.db, log_level=info appears in stdout before connections are accepted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = capture_startup_logs(start_server())
startup_log = find_log_entry(result.stdout, level="info", msg="server starting")
assert startup_log != nil
assert startup_log["bind"] == "0.0.0.0"
assert startup_log["port"] == 8080  // integer, not string
assert startup_log["db_path"] == "./data/af-hub.db"
assert startup_log["log_level"] == "info"
assert count_matching_logs(result.stdout, msg="server starting") == 1

test_cases.description: Server creates the parent directories for the database path automatically if they do not exist before opening the database.
preconditions: Database parent directory ./data does not exist
Server has write permission to the current directory
test_cases.input: Start server with default database path ./data/af-hub.db where ./data does not exist
test_cases.expected: Directory ./data is created; database file ./data/af-hub.db is created and opened; server continues startup without error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ensure_directory_absent("./data")
start_server()
assert directory_exists("./data") == true
assert file_exists("./data/af-hub.db") == true
assert server_started_successfully() == true

test_cases.description: Server applies WAL, foreign_keys, and busy_timeout pragmas in the correct order after opening the database.
preconditions: Database at ./data/af-hub.db is writable
Server is in initialization phase
test_cases.input: Open SQLite database and apply pragmas
test_cases.expected: After initialization: PRAGMA journal_mode returns WAL; PRAGMA foreign_keys returns 1; PRAGMA busy_timeout returns 5000; all three are applied before any table creation
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db = open_sqlite_database("./data/af-hub.db")
apply_pragmas(db)
result_wal = db.query_scalar("PRAGMA journal_mode")
result_fk = db.query_scalar("PRAGMA foreign_keys")
result_bt = db.query_scalar("PRAGMA busy_timeout")
assert result_wal == "wal"
assert result_fk == 1
assert result_bt == 5000

test_cases.description: Server creates all eight tables and five indexes using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS and CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS on boot.
preconditions: Fresh SQLite database at ./data/af-hub.db (newly created or empty)
test_cases.input: Server startup with a fresh database
test_cases.expected: All eight tables (users, admin_tokens, api_keys, teams, team_members, workspaces, workspace_tokens) and all five indexes (idx_api_keys_key_id, idx_workspace_tokens_token_id, idx_users_provider, idx_workspaces_slug, idx_teams_slug) exist in the database; subsequent boots are idempotent (no error on re-creation)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_server_and_stop()
db = open_sqlite_readonly("./data/af-hub.db")
expected_tables = ["users", "admin_tokens", "api_keys", "teams", "team_members", "workspaces", "workspace_tokens"]
for tbl in expected_tables:
  assert table_exists(db, tbl) == true
expected_indexes = ["idx_api_keys_key_id", "idx_workspace_tokens_token_id", "idx_users_provider", "idx_workspaces_slug", "idx_teams_slug"]
for idx in expected_indexes:
  assert index_exists(db, idx) == true
// Second boot must not fail
start_server_and_stop()  // idempotency check
assert no_error_on_second_boot == true

test_cases.description: Server uses Go's database/sql connection pool with default settings and no explicit max-connection overrides.
preconditions: Server is initialized with SQLite database
test_cases.input: Inspect the sql.DB instance after initialization
test_cases.expected: sql.DB instance has no explicit SetMaxOpenConns or SetMaxIdleConns overrides applied; busy_timeout pragma handles write contention at the SQLite layer

test_cases.description: GET /healthz returns HTTP 200 with body {"status": "ok"} without performing any database check.
preconditions: Server is fully started and listening on port 8080
test_cases.input: GET /healthz
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is {"status": "ok"}; no database query is executed as part of this handler

test_cases.description: GET /readyz returns HTTP 200 with body {"status": "ready"} when the database SELECT 1 query succeeds within 2 seconds.
preconditions: Server is fully started; SQLite database is healthy and responsive
test_cases.input: GET /readyz
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is {"status": "ready"}; SELECT 1 was executed against the SQLite connection with a 2-second timeout

test_cases.description: readyz handler uses a package-level atomic int64 counter: first DB failure logs at error level, subsequent failures at debug level, and recovery logs at info level with counter reset.
preconditions: Server is running; database is initially healthy
Ability to simulate DB failure (e.g., close or corrupt the DB connection)
test_cases.input: Simulate DB failure then probe /readyz three times, then restore DB and probe again
test_cases.expected: First probe after failure: error-level log entry emitted, counter=1; second probe after failure: debug-level log entry emitted; third probe after failure: debug-level log entry; first probe after recovery: info-level recovery log entry emitted, counter reset to 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
simulate_db_failure()
response1 = http_get("/readyz")
assert response1.status_code == 503
assert log_contains(level="error", about="db check failure")
assert atomic_read(readyz_failure_counter) == 1

response2 = http_get("/readyz")
assert response2.status_code == 503
assert last_readyz_log_level == "debug"

restore_db()
response3 = http_get("/readyz")
assert response3.status_code == 200
assert log_contains(level="info", about="recovery")
assert atomic_read(readyz_failure_counter) == 0

test_cases.description: Server continues running and accepting connections when /readyz returns HTTP 503; no self-shutdown or state change occurs.
preconditions: Server is running; database connection is intentionally broken to produce 503 from /readyz
test_cases.input: GET /readyz with a broken DB connection; then GET /healthz
test_cases.expected: GET /readyz returns HTTP 503; server remains listening; subsequent GET /healthz returns HTTP 200; server does not shut down or restart
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
simulate_db_failure()
readyz_response = http_get("/readyz")
assert readyz_response.status_code == 503

// Server must still be alive
healthz_response = http_get("/healthz")
assert healthz_response.status_code == 200
assert server_process_is_still_running() == true

test_cases.description: Non-GET/HEAD HTTP methods on /healthz and /readyz return HTTP 405 using Echo's default behavior without a custom JSON error envelope.
preconditions: Server is fully started and listening on port 8080
test_cases.input: POST /healthz and POST /readyz
test_cases.expected: HTTP 405 for both; response body is Echo's default 405 body (not the {"error": {...}} API error envelope)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response_healthz = http_post("http://localhost:8080/healthz", body=nil)
assert response_healthz.status_code == 405
assert not_matches_api_error_envelope(response_healthz.body)

response_readyz = http_post("http://localhost:8080/readyz", body=nil)
assert response_readyz.status_code == 405
assert not_matches_api_error_envelope(response_readyz.body)

test_cases.description: Logrus is configured with JSON formatter, log level from config, and stdout as the sole output destination.
preconditions: Server is initializing logging subsystem with config log.level=debug
test_cases.input: Initialize logging with config specifying debug level
test_cases.expected: logrus global formatter is &logrus.JSONFormatter{}; output is os.Stdout; level is debug; no file or stderr output is configured
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = Config{Log: {Level: "debug"}}
initialize_logging(cfg)
assert logrus.GetFormatter() is instance of JSONFormatter
assert logrus.GetOutput() == os.Stdout
assert logrus.GetLevel() == logrus.DebugLevel
assert no_stderr_writer_configured() == true
assert no_file_writer_configured() == true

test_cases.description: Request logger middleware emits a structured log entry with method, path, status, duration_ms, and request_id for every completed HTTP request.
preconditions: Server is fully started with JSON logging to stdout
test_cases.input: GET /healthz
test_cases.expected: A JSON log entry appears in stdout containing: method=GET (string), path=/healthz (string), status=200 (integer), duration_ms (float > 0), request_id (non-empty UUID string)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = http_get("http://localhost:8080/healthz")
log_entry = find_request_log_entry(stdout, path="/healthz")
assert log_entry["method"] == "GET"
assert log_entry["path"] == "/healthz"
assert log_entry["status"] == 200
assert is_float(log_entry["duration_ms"]) and log_entry["duration_ms"] > 0
assert is_valid_uuid(log_entry["request_id"])

test_cases.description: Server supports all seven logrus log levels including trace which maps to native logrus TraceLevel.
preconditions: logrus v1.4.0 or later is in go.mod
test_cases.input: Set log level to trace; emit a logrus.Trace() entry
test_cases.expected: logrus.ParseLevel("trace") succeeds; logrus.SetLevel(TraceLevel) sets the level; trace-level log entries appear in stdout; no fallback or aliasing occurs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
level, err = logrus.ParseLevel("trace")
assert err == nil
assert level == logrus.TraceLevel
logrus.SetLevel(level)
logrus.Trace("test trace message")
assert log_output_contains_level(stdout, "trace")
// Verify all other levels too
for lvl in ["debug", "info", "warn", "error", "fatal", "panic"]:
  parsed, err = logrus.ParseLevel(lvl)
  assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Request logger middleware accepts a valid X-Request-ID header value and uses it as the request_id.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: GET /healthz with X-Request-ID: my-valid-request-123
test_cases.expected: request_id in the log entry and X-Request-ID response header both equal my-valid-request-123; no new UUID is generated
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = http_get("/healthz", headers={"X-Request-ID": "my-valid-request-123"})
assert response.headers["X-Request-ID"] == "my-valid-request-123"
log_entry = find_request_log_entry(stdout, request_id="my-valid-request-123")
assert log_entry["request_id"] == "my-valid-request-123"

test_cases.description: Request logger middleware generates a UUID v4 as the request_id when no X-Request-ID header is present.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: GET /healthz with no X-Request-ID header
test_cases.expected: X-Request-ID response header is set to a valid UUID v4 string; log entry contains the same UUID v4 as request_id
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = http_get("/healthz")  // no X-Request-ID header
request_id = response.headers["X-Request-ID"]
assert is_valid_uuid_v4(request_id)
log_entry = find_request_log_entry(stdout)
assert log_entry["request_id"] == request_id

test_cases.description: Request logger middleware always sets the X-Request-ID response header on every response regardless of whether the ID was propagated or generated.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: Multiple requests: one with X-Request-ID header, one without, one with an invalid X-Request-ID
test_cases.expected: All three responses include X-Request-ID header; values are non-empty strings
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = http_get("/healthz", headers={"X-Request-ID": "valid-id"})
assert r1.headers["X-Request-ID"] == "valid-id"

r2 = http_get("/healthz")  // no header
assert r2.headers["X-Request-ID"] != ""
assert is_valid_uuid_v4(r2.headers["X-Request-ID"])

r3 = http_get("/healthz", headers={"X-Request-ID": "\x01invalid"})
assert r3.headers["X-Request-ID"] != ""
assert is_valid_uuid_v4(r3.headers["X-Request-ID"])

test_cases.description: Server stops accepting new connections and drains in-flight requests via Echo's e.Shutdown with a 15-second timeout on SIGTERM.
preconditions: Server is fully started with an in-flight long-running request (simulated with a slow handler)
SIGTERM can be sent to the server process
test_cases.input: Send SIGTERM while a request is in-flight
test_cases.expected: Server stops accepting new connections immediately; the in-flight request completes before server exits; shutdown uses a 15-second context timeout; new connection attempts after SIGTERM are refused
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_slow_request_async("/api/v1/slow", duration=5s)
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)

// New connection should be refused
new_conn_result = try_connect(timeout=1s)
assert new_conn_result == "connection refused"

// In-flight request should complete
slow_request_result = wait_for_slow_request()
assert slow_request_result.completed == true

assert server_exited() == true

test_cases.description: Server logs an info-level entry with msg=server shutdown complete and exits cleanly when shutdown completes within 15 seconds.
preconditions: Server is fully started with no long in-flight requests
test_cases.input: Send SIGTERM to the server process
test_cases.expected: Server emits an info-level JSON log entry with msg=server shutdown complete; process exits with code 0; no warn-level timeout message is emitted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)
exit_code = wait_for_exit(server_process)
assert exit_code == 0
assert log_contains(stdout, {"level": "info", "msg": "server shutdown complete"})
assert not log_contains(stdout, {"level": "warn", "msg_contains": "timed out"})

test_cases.description: Server logs a warn-level entry with the timeout message and exits when the 15-second drain timeout expires.
preconditions: Server is running with a simulated handler that holds a connection open longer than 15 seconds
SIGTERM can be sent to the process
test_cases.input: Send SIGTERM while a request that will not complete within 15 seconds is in-flight
test_cases.expected: Server emits a warn-level JSON log entry with msg=graceful shutdown timed out; some connections may have been dropped; process exits; no connection count is included in the message
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_blocking_request_async("/api/v1/slow", blocks_forever=true)
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)
exit = wait_for_exit(server_process, timeout=20s)
assert exit != nil  // process exited
warn_log = find_log_entry(stdout, level="warn", msg="graceful shutdown timed out; some connections may have been dropped")
assert warn_log != nil
assert "connection_count" not in warn_log  // no numeric count

test_cases.description: On first boot (zero rows in admin_tokens), server creates admin user, generates af_admin_ token, stores its SHA-256 hash, and writes plaintext to admin_token file with mode 0600.
preconditions: Fresh database with zero rows in admin_tokens
No AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN env var set
No --reset-admin-token flag
test_cases.input: Start server for the first time
test_cases.expected: users table has a row with username=admin, email=admin@localhost, provider=local, provider_id=admin, status=active; admin_tokens has exactly one row; ./admin_token file exists with mode 0600 containing a token matching af_admin_<64 hex chars> pattern; warn-level log entry with absolute path of admin_token file is emitted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_server_first_boot()
db = open_sqlite("./data/af-hub.db")
user = db.query_one("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = 'admin'")
assert user.email == "admin@localhost"
assert user.provider == "local"
assert user.provider_id == "admin"
assert user.status == "active"

admin_token_row = db.query_one("SELECT * FROM admin_tokens")
assert admin_token_row != nil

token_file_content = read_file("./admin_token")
assert matches_pattern(token_file_content, /^af_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$/)
assert file_mode("./admin_token") == 0600

hash_of_suffix = sha256(token_file_content[9:])  // strip "af_admin_"
assert admin_token_row.token_hash == hex(hash_of_suffix)

warn_log = find_log_entry(stdout, level="warn", msg_contains="admin_token")
assert is_absolute_path(warn_log["path"])

test_cases.description: When AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is set on first boot, server logs an info-level notice and ignores the env var, proceeding with normal first-boot token generation.
preconditions: Fresh database with zero rows in admin_tokens
AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is set to any value in the environment
test_cases.input: Start server with AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN=some_value and zero rows in admin_tokens
test_cases.expected: Info-level log entry with msg=AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is set but will be ignored on first boot; a new token will be generated; new token is generated regardless; env var value is not used
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
set_env("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN", "some_value")
start_server_first_boot()
info_log = find_log_entry(stdout, level="info", msg="AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is set but will be ignored on first boot; a new token will be generated")
assert info_log != nil

// Verify a new token was generated (not the env var value)
new_token = read_file("./admin_token")
assert new_token != "some_value"
assert matches_pattern(new_token, /^af_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$/)

test_cases.description: Server overwrites an existing admin_token file on first boot and logs a warn-level entry with the path.
preconditions: Fresh database with zero rows in admin_tokens
./admin_token file already exists at the target path with old content
test_cases.input: Start server first boot when ./admin_token file already exists
test_cases.expected: File is overwritten with the new token; warn-level log entry with msg=admin_token file already existed and was overwritten and path=<absolute path> is emitted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
write_file("./admin_token", "old_token_value")
start_server_first_boot()
new_content = read_file("./admin_token")
assert new_content != "old_token_value"
assert matches_pattern(new_content, /^af_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$/)

warn_log = find_log_entry(stdout, level="warn", msg="admin_token file already existed and was overwritten")
assert warn_log != nil
assert is_absolute_path(warn_log["path"])

test_cases.description: On subsequent boot, server reads AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN, hashes it, compares to stored hash, and starts only if they match; exits with code 1 if absent or mismatched.
preconditions: admin_tokens table has one row with a known token hash
Correct plaintext token is known
test_cases.input: Two sub-cases: (a) Start with correct AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN; (b) Start with wrong AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN
test_cases.expected: (a) Server starts successfully; (b) Server logs fatal error and exits with code 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// Sub-case (a): correct token
set_env("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN", correct_token)
result_a = start_server()
assert result_a.started_successfully == true

// Sub-case (b): wrong token
set_env("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN", "af_admin_" + "0" * 64)
result_b = run_server_process()
assert result_b.exit_code == 1
assert log_contains(result_b.stdout, {"level": "fatal"})

test_cases.description: Server logs a warn-level security notice when admin_token file is still present on a subsequent boot.
preconditions: admin_tokens has one row; correct AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN set; ./admin_token file still exists at expected path
test_cases.input: Start server on subsequent boot with ./admin_token file present
test_cases.expected: Warn-level log entry with msg=admin_token plaintext file still exists on disk; delete after securing the token and path=<absolute path> is emitted; server continues startup normally
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ensure_file_exists("./admin_token")
set_env("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN", correct_token)
start_server_subsequent_boot()
warn_log = find_log_entry(stdout, level="warn", msg="admin_token plaintext file still exists on disk; delete after securing the token")
assert warn_log != nil
assert is_absolute_path(warn_log["path"])
assert server_started_successfully() == true

test_cases.description: Server emits no log entry about the admin_token file when it is absent on a subsequent boot.
preconditions: admin_tokens has one row; correct AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN set; ./admin_token file does NOT exist
test_cases.input: Start server on subsequent boot without ./admin_token file
test_cases.expected: No log entry of any kind references the absent admin_token file; server starts normally
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ensure_file_absent("./admin_token")
set_env("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN", correct_token)
start_server_subsequent_boot()
logs = collect_all_stdout_logs()
for log in logs:
  assert "admin_token" not in log["msg"].lower() or log["msg"] == "server starting"  // startup info only
assert server_started_successfully() == true

test_cases.description: Server with --reset-admin-token bypasses AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN validation, generates a new token, replaces admin_tokens row, and writes the new plaintext to admin_token file.
preconditions: admin_tokens has one existing row with known hash
AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is not set
--reset-admin-token flag is provided
test_cases.input: Start server with --reset-admin-token flag, no AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN env var
test_cases.expected: New token generated; admin_tokens has exactly one row with new hash; admin_token file contains new plaintext matching af_admin_<64 hex chars>; old token hash is gone; warn-level log with absolute path emitted; server continues to start normally
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
old_hash = get_admin_token_hash_from_db()
run_server_with_flags(["--reset-admin-token"])

db = open_sqlite("./data/af-hub.db")
new_hash = db.query_scalar("SELECT token_hash FROM admin_tokens")
assert new_hash != old_hash
assert db.query_scalar("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM admin_tokens") == 1

new_token = read_file("./admin_token")
assert matches_pattern(new_token, /^af_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$/)

warn_log = find_log_entry(stdout, level="warn", msg_contains="admin_token")
assert is_absolute_path(warn_log["path"])
assert server_started_successfully() == true

test_cases.description: Server with --reset-admin-token on a system with zero rows in admin_tokens behaves identically to first boot.
preconditions: admin_tokens table has zero rows
--reset-admin-token flag is provided
test_cases.input: Start server with --reset-admin-token and empty admin_tokens
test_cases.expected: Token generated and stored; admin_token file written with mode 0600; server starts normally; no special-casing or error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ensure_admin_tokens_empty()
run_server_with_flags(["--reset-admin-token"])
assert file_exists("./admin_token") == true
assert matches_pattern(read_file("./admin_token"), /^af_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$/)
assert file_mode("./admin_token") == 0600
assert db.query_scalar("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM admin_tokens") == 1
assert server_started_successfully() == true

test_cases.description: --reset-admin-token is compatible with --config flag; both flags are processed independently.
preconditions: admin_tokens table has one row
A custom config file exists at /tmp/test-config.toml
test_cases.input: Start server with --reset-admin-token --config /tmp/test-config.toml
test_cases.expected: Server applies the custom config from /tmp/test-config.toml; token rotation completes; admin_token file written in /tmp/ (same directory as config); server starts normally
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
write_file("/tmp/test-config.toml", "[server]\nport = 9191")
run_server_with_flags(["--reset-admin-token", "--config", "/tmp/test-config.toml"])
assert file_exists("/tmp/admin_token") == true
assert matches_pattern(read_file("/tmp/admin_token"), /^af_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$/)
assert server_is_listening_on_port(9191) == true

test_cases.description: Server establishes the correct Echo route group structure: health probes on root, auth group at /api/v1/auth with no auth middleware, protected group at /api/v1 with auth middleware.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: Send requests to /healthz, /api/v1/auth/<any>, and /api/v1/<protected>
test_cases.expected: /healthz accessible without any auth header; /api/v1/auth/test route (if registered) reachable without auth; /api/v1/ routes require auth header

test_cases.description: Auth middleware exclusion for /api/v1/auth/* is enforced via Echo group structure, not path-prefix string matching.
preconditions: Server is fully started; auth group is configured without auth middleware
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/auth/anything without Authorization header
test_cases.expected: Request is not rejected with HTTP 401 by auth middleware; routes under /api/v1/auth/ do not trigger the auth middleware at all

test_cases.description: Global custom error handler translates all errors on non-health-probe routes into the standard JSON error envelope.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/nonexistent-route (triggers 404 from Echo)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with Content-Type application/json; body is {"error": {"code": 404, "message": "Not Found"}} (or similar message from echo.HTTPError)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = http_get("/api/v1/nonexistent")
assert response.status_code == 404
assert response.headers["Content-Type"] contains "application/json"
body = parse_json(response.body)
assert body["error"] != nil
assert body["error"]["code"] == 404
assert body["error"]["message"] != ""

test_cases.description: Custom error handler uses Code and Message from *echo.HTTPError to construct the envelope response.
preconditions: Custom error handler is registered on the Echo instance
test_cases.input: An *echo.HTTPError with Code=422 and Message=unprocessable entity is received by the error handler
test_cases.expected: HTTP 422 response; body is {"error": {"code": 422, "message": "unprocessable entity"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
err = &echo.HTTPError{Code: 422, Message: "unprocessable entity"}
response = invoke_error_handler(err)
assert response.status_code == 422
body = parse_json(response.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 422
assert body["error"]["message"] == "unprocessable entity"

test_cases.description: Custom error handler returns HTTP 500 with the internal server error envelope for non-*echo.HTTPError errors.
preconditions: Custom error handler is registered on the Echo instance
test_cases.input: A plain Go error (errors.New("database connection lost")) is received by the error handler
test_cases.expected: HTTP 500; body is {"error": {"code": 500, "message": "internal server error"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
err = errors.New("database connection lost")
response = invoke_error_handler(err)
assert response.status_code == 500
body = parse_json(response.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 500
assert body["error"]["message"] == "internal server error"

test_cases.description: Recover middleware catches panics and produces HTTP 500 with the standard error envelope; no raw panic output reaches the client.
preconditions: Server has a test route registered that panics when called
Server is fully started
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/panic-route (handler panics)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 500; body is {"error": {"code": 500, "message": "internal server error"}}; no raw panic stack trace in response body; X-Request-ID header is present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
register_panic_handler("/api/v1/panic-test")
response = http_get("/api/v1/panic-test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + valid_admin_token})
assert response.status_code == 500
body = parse_json(response.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 500
assert body["error"]["message"] == "internal server error"
assert "panic" not in response.body.lower()  // no raw panic in body
assert response.headers["X-Request-ID"] != ""

test_cases.description: Health probe routes return plain JSON status objects, not the error envelope; error envelope is never applied to these routes.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: GET /healthz and GET /readyz
test_cases.expected: Bodies are {"status": "ok"} and {"status": "ready"} respectively; neither is wrapped in {"error": {...}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = http_get("/healthz")
assert parse_json(r1.body) == {"status": "ok"}
assert "error" not in parse_json(r1.body)

r2 = http_get("/readyz")
assert parse_json(r2.body) in [{"status": "ready"}, {"status": "not ready"}]
assert "error" not in parse_json(r2.body)

test_cases.description: Middleware executes in the order: Recover → body-size limit → request logger → auth for protected routes.
preconditions: Server is fully started with middleware registered in the correct order
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/some-endpoint with a body exceeding 1 MB and a valid auth token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 413 is returned (body-size limit fires before auth); the 413 appears in the request log; no auth processing occurs after 413
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
large_body = generate_bytes(2 * 1024 * 1024)  // 2 MB
response = http_post("/api/v1/some-endpoint",
  headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + valid_admin_token},
  body=large_body)
assert response.status_code == 413
log_entry = find_request_log_entry(stdout)
assert log_entry["status"] == 413
// Auth context should NOT be set (body-size limit fired before auth)
// Verified by checking the handler was never reached

test_cases.description: Body-size limit middleware returns HTTP 413 immediately for request bodies exceeding 1 MB; request logger captures the 413 status.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/some-endpoint with a 1.1 MB request body and valid admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 413 with body {"error": {"code": 413, "message": "Request Entity Too Large"}} (or echo's default message); log entry has status=413
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
body = generate_bytes(1100 * 1024)  // 1.1 MB
response = http_post("/api/v1/test",
  headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + valid_admin_token},
  body=body)
assert response.status_code == 413
body_json = parse_json(response.body)
assert body_json["error"]["code"] == 413
log_entry = find_request_log_entry(stdout)
assert log_entry["status"] == 413

test_cases.description: Body-size limit middleware is a no-op for GET and HEAD requests with no body.
preconditions: Server is fully started
test_cases.input: GET /healthz and HEAD /healthz
test_cases.expected: Both return their expected status codes (200); no HTTP 413 is issued; requests proceed normally
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r_get = http_get("/healthz")
assert r_get.status_code == 200

r_head = http_head("/healthz")
assert r_head.status_code == 200

// No 413 in either case
assert r_get.status_code != 413
assert r_head.status_code != 413

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with the standard envelope for all five missing/invalid token conditions.
preconditions: Server is fully started; a protected route /api/v1/test is available
test_cases.input: Five requests: (a) no Authorization header, (b) Authorization: Basic foo, (c) Authorization: Bearer <empty>, (d) Authorization: Bearer totally-wrong, (e) Authorization: Bearer af_12345 (malformed)
test_cases.expected: All five return HTTP 401 with {"error": {"code": 401, "message": "missing or invalid authentication credentials"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cases = [
  {},  // (a) no header
  {"Authorization": "Basic foo"},  // (b) wrong scheme
  {"Authorization": "Bearer "},  // (c) empty token
  {"Authorization": "Bearer totally-wrong"},  // (d) unrecognized prefix
  {"Authorization": "Bearer af_12345"},  // (e) structural failure
]
for headers in cases:
  r = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers=headers)
  assert r.status_code == 401
  body = parse_json(r.body)
  assert body["error"]["code"] == 401
  assert body["error"]["message"] == "missing or invalid authentication credentials"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware accepts a valid admin token, sets AuthContext with CredentialType=admin, IsAdmin=true, UserID=empty, WorkspaceID=empty.
preconditions: Server is fully started; admin_tokens has one row with known token hash; a protected route is available
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/test with Authorization: Bearer af_admin_<64 valid hex chars>
test_cases.expected: Request reaches the handler; AuthContext in Echo context has CredentialType=admin, IsAdmin=true, UserID="", WorkspaceID=""; handler returns success
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token = read_file("./admin_token").trim()
response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
assert response.status_code != 401
assert response.status_code != 403
// Verify via a test handler that echoes AuthContext:
ctx_data = parse_json(response.body)
assert ctx_data["credential_type"] == "admin"
assert ctx_data["is_admin"] == true
assert ctx_data["user_id"] == ""
assert ctx_data["workspace_id"] == ""

test_cases.description: Auth middleware validates and authenticates a user API key, sets AuthContext with CredentialType=api_key and the user's UUID.
preconditions: A user exists in users table with status=active; a corresponding api_keys row exists with known key_id and secret; secret_hash=SHA256(secret); no expiry or revocation
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/test with Authorization: Bearer af_<key_id>_<secret>
test_cases.expected: AuthContext has CredentialType=api_key, UserID=<user UUID>, IsAdmin=false, WorkspaceID=""
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
key_id = "abcd1234"  // 8 alphanumeric
secret = "A" * 32  // 32 alphanumeric
token = "af_" + key_id + "_" + secret
insert_api_key(db, key_id, sha256(secret), user_id)
response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
assert response.status_code != 401
assert response.status_code != 403
ctx = parse_auth_context(response)
assert ctx.credential_type == "api_key"
assert ctx.user_id == user_uuid
assert ctx.is_admin == false
assert ctx.workspace_id == ""

test_cases.description: Auth middleware validates and authenticates a workspace token, sets AuthContext with CredentialType=workspace_token, UserID, and WorkspaceID.
preconditions: User exists in users with status=active; workspace exists in workspaces; workspace_tokens row exists with known token_id and secret; no expiry or revocation
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/test with Authorization: Bearer af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>
test_cases.expected: AuthContext has CredentialType=workspace_token, UserID=<user UUID>, WorkspaceID=<workspace UUID>, IsAdmin=false
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token_id = "wt123456"  // 8 alphanumeric
secret = "B" * 32  // 32 alphanumeric
token = "af_wt_" + token_id + "_" + secret
insert_workspace_token(db, token_id, sha256(secret), workspace_id, user_id)
response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
assert response.status_code != 401
assert response.status_code != 403
ctx = parse_auth_context(response)
assert ctx.credential_type == "workspace_token"
assert ctx.user_id == user_uuid
assert ctx.workspace_id == workspace_uuid
assert ctx.is_admin == false

test_cases.description: AuthContext is stored in Echo context under typed key AuthContextKey and is retrievable by downstream handlers.
preconditions: Auth middleware has successfully authenticated a request
test_cases.input: A handler calls c.Get(string(AuthContextKey)).(*AuthContext) after auth middleware runs
test_cases.expected: The retrieved *AuthContext is non-nil and has correct fields matching the authenticated identity

test_cases.description: Auth middleware performs all structural validation before any DB query for all three credential types.
preconditions: Server is fully started; DB is accessible but no api_keys or workspace_tokens rows exist
test_cases.input: Structurally invalid tokens: af_tooshort_<32 chars>, af_wt_toolong1234_<32 chars>, af_admin_notHex
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401 returned immediately for each; no DB queries are executed (verify via DB query log or mock)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
invalid_tokens = [
  "af_tooshort_" + "A" * 32,  // key_id too short
  "af_wt_toolong1234_" + "B" * 32,  // token_id too long
  "af_admin_notvalidhexstr_too_short",  // admin token wrong length
]
for token in invalid_tokens:
  db_query_count_before = get_db_query_count()
  response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
  db_query_count_after = get_db_query_count()
  assert response.status_code == 401
  assert db_query_count_after == db_query_count_before  // no DB queries

test_cases.description: Auth middleware uses crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare for all hash comparisons to prevent timing attacks.
preconditions: Auth middleware source code is reviewable; or a test harness can intercept comparison calls
test_cases.input: Auth middleware source inspection or instrumented comparison for admin token, API key, and workspace token verification
test_cases.expected: All three credential verification paths use subtle.ConstantTimeCompare for hash comparison; no direct byte slice equality (bytes.Equal) is used for hash comparison

test_cases.description: Admin token authentication bypasses the blocked-user check entirely; IsAdmin is true and UserID is empty.
preconditions: admin user row in users has status=blocked (manually updated); admin_tokens has the correct hash; server is started with correct AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/test with a valid admin token when the admin user row has status=blocked
test_cases.expected: HTTP 2xx from the handler (not 403); AuthContext has IsAdmin=true and UserID=""; no users table lookup occurred for admin token auth
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.exec("UPDATE users SET status = 'blocked' WHERE username = 'admin'")
token = read_file("./admin_token").trim()
response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
assert response.status_code != 403
assert response.status_code != 401
ctx = parse_auth_context(response)
assert ctx.is_admin == true
assert ctx.user_id == ""

property_tests.description: For any admin token of format af_admin_<64 hex chars>, the stored hash in admin_tokens.token_hash is always the SHA-256 hex digest of the 64 hex-char suffix.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate 100 random 64-char hex strings as admin token suffixes
property_tests.invariant_check: for each suffix: token = 'af_admin_' + suffix; stored_hash = store_and_retrieve_admin_token(token); expected_hash = hex(sha256(suffix)); assert stored_hash == expected_hash; at verification time: recomputed_hash = hex(sha256(suffix)); assert constant_time_compare(recomputed_hash, stored_hash) == true

property_tests.description: For any user API key, the SHA-256 hash is computed from the raw 32-alphanumeric-char secret component only and compared via constant-time comparison.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate 100 random (key_id: 8 alphanumeric chars, secret: 32 alphanumeric chars) pairs
property_tests.invariant_check: for each pair: token = 'af_' + key_id + '_' + secret; secret_hash = hex(sha256(secret)); insert api_key with this hash; invoke auth middleware with token; assert middleware computes sha256 of secret only (not full token, not key_id); assert comparison uses constant_time_compare; assert result is authenticated

property_tests.description: For any workspace token, the SHA-256 hash is computed from the raw 32-alphanumeric-char secret component only and compared via constant-time comparison.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate 100 random (token_id: 8 alphanumeric chars, secret: 32 alphanumeric chars) pairs
property_tests.invariant_check: for each pair: token = 'af_wt_' + token_id + '_' + secret; secret_hash = hex(sha256(secret)); insert workspace_token with this hash; invoke auth middleware with token; assert middleware computes sha256 of secret only (not full token, not token_id); assert comparison uses constant_time_compare; assert result is authenticated

property_tests.description: For any request authenticated via admin token, AuthContext.IsAdmin is always true, UserID is always empty string, and no users table query is ever executed.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate 50 random valid admin tokens; vary users table state (empty, admin row present, admin row blocked, admin row absent)
property_tests.invariant_check: for each token and users state: auth_context = run_auth_middleware(token); assert auth_context.is_admin == true; assert auth_context.user_id == ''; assert auth_context.workspace_id == ''; assert db_users_query_count == 0; assert response_code != 403

property_tests.description: For any token presented to auth middleware, no DB query is executed until the token has passed full structural validation.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate 200 random strings: mix of valid-prefix-invalid-structure tokens and completely invalid tokens
property_tests.invariant_check: for each token_string: db_queries_before = get_db_query_count(); result = run_auth_middleware(token_string); if result.status == 401 and not_structurally_valid(token_string): assert get_db_query_count() == db_queries_before; // no DB queries were made for structurally invalid tokens

property_tests.description: For any server boot invocation, the HTTP listener never opens before SQLite initialization and admin bootstrap/validation have both completed.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: run 20 server boot cycles with varying config and environment (first boot, subsequent boot, reset flag)
property_tests.invariant_check: for each boot: record timestamps of (db_init_complete, admin_bootstrap_complete, http_listener_open); assert http_listener_open > admin_bootstrap_complete; assert admin_bootstrap_complete > db_init_complete; assert health_probe_unreachable_before_listener_open()

property_tests.description: For any error response from a route under /api/v1/*, the body always matches the envelope {"error": {"code": <integer>, "message": "<string>"}} where code equals the HTTP status code.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: trigger 10 different error conditions on /api/v1/* routes: 401 (no auth), 403 (blocked user), 404 (not found), 413 (body too large), 500 (panic), 503 (db contention)
property_tests.invariant_check: for each error_response: body = parse_json(error_response.body); assert 'error' in body; assert 'code' in body['error']; assert 'message' in body['error']; assert body['error']['code'] == error_response.status_code; assert type(body['error']['code']) == integer; assert type(body['error']['message']) == string; assert body['error']['message'] != ''

property_tests.description: For any number of concurrent GET /readyz requests, the failure counter is always a consistent int64 readable by any goroutine with no data races.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: run 50 concurrent /readyz requests in goroutines under various DB health states (healthy, degraded, recovering)
property_tests.invariant_check: run all concurrent requests with Go race detector enabled (-race flag); assert no data race is detected on the failure counter; assert counter value after each successful probe is 0; assert counter value after each failed probe is >= 1; assert counter never becomes negative

property_tests.description: The admin_token file is always written with mode 0600 on any first boot or --reset-admin-token rotation.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: perform 20 iterations: alternate between first boot and rotation scenarios on varying filesystems
property_tests.invariant_check: for each iteration: trigger admin_token file write; file_info = stat('./admin_token'); assert file_info.mode().perm() == 0600; assert file_info.mode() & 0040 == 0; // no group read; assert file_info.mode() & 0004 == 0; // no world read

property_tests.description: Token expiry uses an exclusive UTC boundary: a token is expired iff expires_at < current UTC time; a token with expires_at equal to now is not expired.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate 50 tokens with expires_at values spanning: past (various durations), exact now, near future (1ms, 1s, 1h ahead)
property_tests.invariant_check: for each token: now = utc_now(); result = check_token_expiry(token, now); if expires_at < now: assert result == EXPIRED; if expires_at == now: assert result == VALID; if expires_at > now: assert result == VALID; // exclusive lower boundary only

edge_case_tests.description: Missing config.toml at the resolved path is non-fatal; server applies all defaults and starts normally.
preconditions: No config.toml file exists at ./config.toml
No --config flag is provided
edge_case_tests.expected: Server starts normally; all defaults apply (port=8080, bind=0.0.0.0, db=./data/af-hub.db, level=info); no error or fatal log entry about missing config
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
ensure_file_absent("./config.toml")
result = start_server()
assert result.started_successfully == true
assert result.config.port == 8080
assert result.config.bind == "0.0.0.0"
assert result.config.db_path == "./data/af-hub.db"
assert result.config.log_level == "info"
assert not log_contains(result.stdout, {"level": "fatal"})
assert not log_contains(result.stdout, {"level": "error", "msg_contains": "config"})

edge_case_tests.description: admin_token file is always written to the directory containing the resolved config.toml; with no --config flag it goes to the current working directory.
preconditions: No --config flag is provided; no config.toml exists
First boot (zero rows in admin_tokens)
edge_case_tests.input: Start server without --config flag; first boot
edge_case_tests.expected: ./admin_token file is created in the current working directory regardless of whether config.toml exists
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
cwd = get_current_working_directory()
start_server_first_boot()
assert file_exists(cwd + "/admin_token") == true
assert is_absolute_path(get_warn_log_path(stdout)) == true
assert warn_log_path == cwd + "/admin_token"

edge_case_tests.description: Any fatal error during initialization steps 2-5 causes the server to log a fatal entry and exit with code 1 before opening the HTTP listener.
preconditions: Server is configured to open a database at a path where the directory cannot be created (e.g., /root/noperm/db.db) to simulate a fatal init error
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with code 1; fatal-level JSON log entry is emitted with the failure reason; no HTTP connections are accepted on any port
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
write_config("./config.toml", database_path="/root/noperm/db.db")
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code == 1
assert log_contains(result.stdout, {"level": "fatal"})
assert http_port_not_open(8080) == true

edge_case_tests.description: Server logs a fatal error and exits with code 1 when the parent directory for the database path cannot be created.
preconditions: The database parent directory path is read-only (e.g., /root/no-write/af-hub.db)
edge_case_tests.input: Start server with database path whose parent directory is unwritable
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with code 1; fatal-level log entry includes directory creation failure reason; no database file is opened
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
write_config("./config.toml", database_path="/root/no-write/af-hub.db")
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code == 1
fatal_log = find_log_entry(result.stdout, level="fatal")
assert fatal_log != nil
assert "permission" in fatal_log["msg"].lower() or "mkdir" in fatal_log["msg"].lower()

edge_case_tests.description: Server logs a fatal error and exits with code 1 when the database file cannot be opened or created.
preconditions: Database path exists but is a directory (not a file), causing open failure
edge_case_tests.input: Start server where database path is an existing directory
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with code 1; fatal-level log entry with open failure reason; no retries; HTTP listener not started
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
create_directory("./data/af-hub.db")  // make it a directory, not a file
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code == 1
fatal_log = find_log_entry(result.stdout, level="fatal")
assert fatal_log != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Server logs a fatal error identifying the specific failing PRAGMA and exits with code 1 when a PRAGMA statement fails.
preconditions: SQLite database is opened on a read-only filesystem causing PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL to fail
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with code 1; fatal-level log entry names the specific PRAGMA that failed (e.g., journal_mode) and the reason
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Simulate via mock or read-only DB
db = open_readonly_sqlite_db()
result = apply_pragmas_with_error_on_first(db)
assert result.exit_code == 1
fatal_log = find_log_entry(stdout, level="fatal")
assert "journal_mode" in fatal_log["msg"] or fatal_log["pragma"] == "journal_mode"

edge_case_tests.description: HTTP 503 with the standard error envelope is returned when a SQLite write operation exhausts the busy_timeout window.
preconditions: Server is running; a write contention condition is simulated (another connection holds a write lock for > 5 seconds)
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/some-endpoint that triggers a DB write while write lock is held by another connection
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 503 with body {"error": {"code": 503, "message": "service temporarily unavailable"}}; server remains running
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
hold_write_lock(db, duration=10s)  // simulate contention
response = http_post("/api/v1/write-endpoint", headers={auth_header})
assert response.status_code == 503
body = parse_json(response.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 503
assert body["error"]["message"] == "service temporarily unavailable"
assert server_still_running() == true

edge_case_tests.description: Two concurrent /readyz probes both succeeding after a degraded period may both log the recovery message; duplicate recovery entries are acceptable.
preconditions: readyz failure counter > 0 (server was in degraded state)
DB is restored and will respond successfully
edge_case_tests.input: Two concurrent GET /readyz requests after DB recovery
edge_case_tests.expected: Both return HTTP 200; at most two info-level recovery log entries appear; counter is reset to 0; no panic or race condition occurs
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Put server in degraded state
simulate_db_failure()
http_get("/readyz")  // increments counter to >= 1
restore_db()

// Fire two concurrent probes
results = concurrent_http_get(["/readyz", "/readyz"], concurrency=2)
for r in results:
  assert r.status_code == 200

recovery_logs = filter_logs(stdout, level="info", msg_contains="recovery")
assert 1 <= len(recovery_logs) <= 2  // at most duplicate
assert atomic_read(readyz_failure_counter) == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Invalid X-Request-ID header values are silently discarded and replaced with a fresh UUID v4; no error is returned.
preconditions: Server is fully started
edge_case_tests.input: Three requests with invalid X-Request-ID values: (a) empty string, (b) non-printable bytes (0x01), (c) 129-character string
edge_case_tests.expected: All three return their normal response with X-Request-ID set to a fresh UUID v4; no HTTP error is returned for any of them
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
invalid_ids = [
  "",  // empty
  "\x01\x02",  // non-printable
  "a" * 129,  // exceeds 128 chars
]
for bad_id in invalid_ids:
  response = http_get("/healthz", headers={"X-Request-ID": bad_id})
  assert response.status_code == 200
  returned_id = response.headers["X-Request-ID"]
  assert returned_id != bad_id
  assert is_valid_uuid_v4(returned_id)

edge_case_tests.description: Hijacked connections (e.g., WebSocket) are closed immediately by http.Server.Shutdown without waiting for them to drain; server shutdown proceeds.
preconditions: Server has an active WebSocket connection when SIGTERM is received
edge_case_tests.input: Send SIGTERM while a WebSocket connection is hijacked
edge_case_tests.expected: Hijacked connection is closed immediately; server shutdown completes within the 15-second window; no special error or hang occurs
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
ws_conn = open_websocket_connection("/api/v1/ws")
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)
exit_result = wait_for_exit(server_process, timeout=18s)
assert exit_result.exited == true
assert exit_result.elapsed_seconds < 16  // within 15s window
assert ws_conn.is_closed() == true

edge_case_tests.description: Server exits with code 1 when the admin_token file cannot be written during first boot.
preconditions: admin_tokens table has zero rows
The directory where admin_token should be written has no write permission
edge_case_tests.input: First boot with unwritable current working directory
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with code 1; fatal-level log entry with write failure reason; no HTTP listener started
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
make_directory_unwritable(".")
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code == 1
fatal_log = find_log_entry(result.stdout, level="fatal")
assert fatal_log != nil
assert "permission" in fatal_log["msg"].lower() or "admin_token" in fatal_log["msg"].lower()

edge_case_tests.description: Server starts normally when admin_tokens has rows but the admin user row in users is missing.
preconditions: admin_tokens has one row with a valid hash; users table has no row for the admin user; correct AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN set
edge_case_tests.input: Subsequent boot with admin_tokens populated but admin user row deleted from users
edge_case_tests.expected: Server starts successfully; admin token auth works without users table lookup; no fatal error
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db.exec("DELETE FROM users WHERE username = 'admin'")
set_env("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN", correct_token)
result = start_server()
assert result.started_successfully == true

// Admin auth still works
response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + correct_token})
assert response.status_code != 401
assert response.status_code != 403

edge_case_tests.description: Server logs a fatal error and exits with code 1 when AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is absent on a subsequent boot.
preconditions: admin_tokens has one row; AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is NOT set in the environment
edge_case_tests.input: Start server on subsequent boot without AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN env var
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with code 1; fatal-level log entry indicates missing env var; no HTTP listener opened
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
unset_env("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN")
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code == 1
fatal_log = find_log_entry(result.stdout, level="fatal")
assert fatal_log != nil
assert http_port_not_open(8080) == true

edge_case_tests.description: Server exits with code 1 when admin_token file cannot be written during --reset-admin-token rotation.
preconditions: admin_tokens has one row
--reset-admin-token flag is provided
Target directory for admin_token file is unwritable
edge_case_tests.input: Start server with --reset-admin-token flag when admin_token file destination is unwritable
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with code 1; fatal-level log entry with write failure reason; server does not start with the new unwritten token
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
make_directory_unwritable(".")
result = run_server_process(flags=["--reset-admin-token"])
assert result.exit_code == 1
fatal_log = find_log_entry(result.stdout, level="fatal")
assert fatal_log != nil
assert http_port_not_open(8080) == true

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 503 when a DB lookup for api_keys or workspace_tokens encounters a write-contention busy-timeout error.
preconditions: Server is running; SQLite write lock is held; a valid-structure API key token is presented
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/test with valid-structure API key while DB write lock is held beyond busy_timeout
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
hold_write_lock(db, duration=10s)
response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer af_abcd1234_" + "A" * 32})
assert response.status_code == 503
body = parse_json(response.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 503
assert body["error"]["message"] == "service temporarily unavailable"

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 immediately without DB query when workspace token remainder splits into wrong number of parts or fails length/charset checks.
preconditions: Server is fully started
edge_case_tests.input: Three malformed workspace tokens: (a) af_wt_abc_def_extra (3 parts after prefix), (b) af_wt_short_<32 chars> (token_id < 8 chars), (c) af_wt_abcd1234_<31 chars> (secret too short)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 401 for all three; no DB query executed
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
invalid_wt_tokens = [
  "af_wt_abc_def_extra",  // too many parts
  "af_wt_short_" + "A" * 32,  // token_id < 8
  "af_wt_abcd1234_" + "B" * 31,  // secret < 32
]
for token in invalid_wt_tokens:
  db_count_before = get_db_query_count()
  response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
  assert response.status_code == 401
  assert body_is_auth_error_envelope(response.body)
  assert get_db_query_count() == db_count_before

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 immediately without DB query when user API key splits into wrong part count or key_id/secret fail structural checks.
preconditions: Server is fully started
edge_case_tests.input: Three malformed API key tokens: (a) af_tooshort_<32 chars> (key_id < 8), (b) af_abcd1234 (missing secret), (c) af_abcd1234_<31 chars> (secret < 32)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 401 for all three; no DB query executed
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
invalid_api_tokens = [
  "af_short_" + "A" * 32,  // key_id too short (5 chars)
  "af_abcd1234",  // missing secret part
  "af_abcd1234_" + "B" * 31,  // secret too short
]
for token in invalid_api_tokens:
  db_count_before = get_db_query_count()
  response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
  assert response.status_code == 401
  assert body["error"]["code"] == 401
  assert body["error"]["message"] == "missing or invalid authentication credentials"
  assert get_db_query_count() == db_count_before

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 for revoked tokens; response is identical to expired token response with no distinction between states.
preconditions: An api_keys row exists with revoked_at set to a non-null timestamp
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/test with a valid-structure API key whose api_keys row has revoked_at set
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 401 with {"error": {"code": 401, "message": "missing or invalid authentication credentials"}}; same response as for expired token
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
revoke_api_key(db, key_id, revoked_at=now())
token = "af_" + key_id + "_" + secret
response = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token})
assert response.status_code == 401
body = parse_json(response.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 401
assert body["error"]["message"] == "missing or invalid authentication credentials"

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 for expired tokens; a token whose expires_at equals the exact current UTC time is NOT expired (exclusive boundary).
preconditions: An api_keys row exists with expires_at set to a past timestamp (expired) and another row with expires_at set to exactly now (boundary)
edge_case_tests.input: Two requests: (a) token with expires_at in the past; (b) token with expires_at equal to exact current UTC instant
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// (a) expired token
past_time = utc_now() - duration(1, "second")
insert_api_key(db, key_id_a, secret_hash_a, user_id, expires_at=past_time)
token_a = "af_" + key_id_a + "_" + secret_a
response_a = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token_a})
assert response_a.status_code == 401

// (b) boundary token (expires_at == now; exclusive, so NOT expired)
boundary_time = utc_now()
insert_api_key(db, key_id_b, secret_hash_b, user_id, expires_at=boundary_time)
token_b = "af_" + key_id_b + "_" + secret_b
response_b = http_get("/api/v1/test", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + token_b})
assert response_b.status_code != 401  // still valid at boundary

smoke_tests.description: Full first-boot server startup: no config, no env vars; server generates admin token, writes file, opens HTTP listener.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run bin/af-hub in a clean directory with no config.toml and no AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN
expected_effects: Process starts without exiting
Directory ./data is created
SQLite database ./data/af-hub.db is created with all 7 tables and 5 indexes
WAL, foreign_keys, and busy_timeout pragmas are applied
users table has one row: username=admin, email=admin@localhost
admin_tokens table has one row
./admin_token file exists with mode 0600 containing af_admin_<64 hex chars>
warn-level log entry with absolute path of admin_token file appears in stdout
info-level startup log with bind, port, db_path, log_level appears in stdout
GET /healthz returns HTTP 200 with {"status": "ok"}
GET /readyz returns HTTP 200 with {"status": "ready"}

smoke_tests.description: Subsequent-boot token validation: server reads AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN, validates hash, and starts normally.
smoke_tests.trigger: Set AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN from the admin_token file and restart bin/af-hub
real_components: Existing SQLite database at ./data/af-hub.db (with admin_tokens row from first boot)
expected_effects: Process starts without exiting
admin_tokens row count is >= 1 (subsequent boot detected)
SHA-256 of AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN matches stored hash
If ./admin_token file is present: warn-level security notice log entry with path field
info-level startup log entry appears with all four fields
GET /healthz returns HTTP 200
GET /readyz returns HTTP 200

smoke_tests.description: Admin token rotation via --reset-admin-token: new token generated, old token invalidated, server starts.
real_components: Existing SQLite database at ./data/af-hub.db with previous admin_tokens row
expected_effects: No AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN validation is performed (env var not read)
admin_tokens table has exactly one row after rotation (old deleted, new inserted)
New token hash differs from old hash
./admin_token file overwritten with new af_admin_<64 hex chars> token
File mode is 0600
warn-level log entry with new file absolute path appears in stdout
Server starts and responds to GET /healthz with HTTP 200
Old admin token is rejected with HTTP 401 on subsequent API requests

smoke_tests.description: Authenticated API request using admin token: middleware resolves identity and sets AuthContext with IsAdmin=true.
smoke_tests.trigger: GET /api/v1/test with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token from file>
mockable: The /api/v1/test handler (registers a test echo handler that returns AuthContext fields)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 (or handler's success code) is returned
AuthContext has CredentialType=admin, IsAdmin=true, UserID="", WorkspaceID=""
X-Request-ID response header is set
Request log entry appears in stdout with method=GET, path=/api/v1/test, status=200, duration_ms>0, request_id=UUID
No users table query is executed during admin token auth

smoke_tests.description: Blocked user is rejected with HTTP 403 when authenticating via user API key.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /api/v1/some-endpoint with valid API key token whose associated user has status=blocked
expected_effects: HTTP 403 with body {"error": {"code": 403, "message": "forbidden"}}
Request log entry has status=403
X-Request-ID header is present in response

smoke_tests.description: Readiness probe returns HTTP 503 in degraded database state and logs appropriately.
smoke_tests.trigger: Simulate DB failure (close connection or lock DB); then GET /readyz
mockable: SQLite connection (to simulate SELECT 1 timeout or failure)
expected_effects: GET /readyz returns HTTP 503 with {"status": "not ready"}
First failure: error-level log entry emitted
readyz_failure_counter incremented to 1
Server remains listening; GET /healthz still returns HTTP 200
No server shutdown initiated

smoke_tests.description: Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM: server drains in-flight requests and exits cleanly within 15 seconds.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send SIGTERM to running bin/af-hub process
expected_effects: Server stops accepting new connections after SIGTERM
In-flight requests are given up to 15 seconds to complete
info-level log entry with msg=server shutdown complete appears in stdout
Process exits with code 0 (if drain completes within 15s)
No warn-level timeout message appears (if drain completes in time)

smoke_tests.description: Oversized request body is rejected with HTTP 413 before auth or handler processing; status 413 appears in the log.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /api/v1/some-endpoint with a 2 MB request body and valid admin token
expected_effects: HTTP 413 returned
Body is {"error": {"code": 413, "message": "Request Entity Too Large"}} (or echo's equivalent)
Request log entry has status=413
Handler is never invoked (body-size limit fires first)
X-Request-ID header is present in the response
